dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Aethelburg, Cycle 237. Another dawn, another cycle.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The city hums, a symphony of progress and precision. Every citizen, every cog, perfectly placed.

dialogue.speaker: SYSTEM VOICE
dialogue.line: Good morning, Citizen Ren. Cycle 237, 06:00. Optimal wake-up achieved. Hydration protocol initiated.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: (Yawn) Optimal, huh? Feels pretty standard to me.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: HISS (of a water dispenser)

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Another day of processing data streams for the Central Archive. Thrilling.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Ren downs the nutrient paste, the same taste, the same texture, every single morning. Aethelburg thrives on consistency.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CLICK-WHIRR (of apartment door sealing)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The sky-lanes are already bustling. Hover-pods glide silently, ferrying citizens to their designated sectors.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Look at them. All plugged in. All content. Sometimes I wonder what they dream about.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: WHOOSH (of a passing hover-pod)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Ren boards the Sector 7 Monorail. The familiar drone, the familiar faces, all staring blankly at their personal comm-pads.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Same old view. Towering chrome, endless glass. It's beautiful, in a sterile, suffocating way.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: HUMMMMM (of the monorail)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: At the Central Archive, the air is thick with the scent of ozone and recycled air. Rows upon rows of data processing units, each manned by a silent worker.

dialogue.speaker: Supervisor Kael
dialogue.line: Citizen Ren. On time, as expected. Proceed to Station Gamma-9.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Yes, Supervisor.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Hours pass. The rhythmic click of data entry, the soft glow of the screen. Ren processes historical records, filtering out anomalies, ensuring narrative consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Another 'discrepancy' flagged. A minor architectural detail in a pre-Unity era building. Easily corrected. Just a stray pixel, probably.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: BEEP (of data input)

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Wait.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Ren pauses. A flicker on the screen. Not a pixel, but... a shadow. A fleeting, impossible shadow in a historical image of a bustling market street.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: What was that? Did I imagine it?

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: ZAP! (a brief visual distortion on screen)

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: There! It happened again! A glitch. But the system didn't flag it.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Ren zooms in, trying to pinpoint the anomaly. The image is crisp, clear. No shadow. No distortion.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Am I tired? Too many cycles without proper rest? No, I felt it. A momentary shift.

dialogue.speaker: Supervisor Kael
dialogue.line: Citizen Ren. Is there an issue with your terminal?

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: No, Supervisor. Just... a momentary lag.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Ren forces the thought away, returning to the mundane task. But the unease lingers. A seed of doubt planted in the perfectly ordered garden of Aethelburg.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Later, walking home through the illuminated sky-paths, Ren glances down at the city's lower levels. The industrial sectors, rarely seen, always shrouded in a permanent twilight.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Just a glitch. That's all it was. The system is flawless.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Then, Ren stops dead. Below, in a dark alley between two towering structures, a figure. Not a citizen in a standard uniform. Not a drone. Something... else.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: GASP (from Ren)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The figure looks up. Its eyes, glowing with an unnatural light, meet Ren's across the vast chasm of the city. For a split second, Ren sees not a reflection, but a mirror. A parallel.

dialogue.speaker: Ren
dialogue.line: Who... what are you?

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: THUMP-THUMP (Ren's heart)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The figure vanishes, swallowed by the shadows. Leaving Ren alone, staring at an empty alley, and a city that suddenly feels very, very wrong.

summary: Ren, a data archivist in the perfectly ordered city of Aethelburg, experiences a subtle glitch in a historical record, sparking unease. Later, Ren witnesses a mysterious figure in the city's lower levels, hinting at a hidden reality.
